segments.text:
GOLGI FUNCTIONS
Sorting of secreted proteins, transmembrane proteins, and lysosomal enzymes
Processing of above proteins
Camillo Golgi
Signal sequences
Converted into active form
- glycosolation

GOLGI ORGANIZATION
Stacks of flat sacs (cisternae)

SEPARATE COMPARTMENTS
Different enzymes within each Golgi compartment

TRANSPORT BETWEEN ER AND GOLGI
COPII-coated vesicles to Golgi
COPI-coated vesicles to ER

LOADING CARGO INTO COPII VESICLE
Soluble cargo binds to membrane receptor
Transmembrane cargo binds to coat directly

SOLUBLE CARGO
Soluble cargo contains FPF signal sequence
FPF binds to Surf4 (a transmembrane receptor)

TRANSMEMBRANE CARGO
Cytosolic domain contains di-acidic signal sequence (DXE)
DXE binds to Sec24 of coat
DXE

RETRIEVAL OF ER PROTEINS
v-SNARES, receptors, ER resident proteins in COPII vesicles
Signal sequences again
Return to ER in COPI-coated vesicles

GOLGI TO ER SIGNAL SEQUENCES
KDEL: at C-terminus of ER soluble proteins
KKXX: at C-terminus of ER transmembrane proteins

REGULATION OF KDEL BINDING
KDEL/receptor binding is pH dependent
Lower pH in Golgi promotes binding

MOVEMENT THROUGH GOLGI
Cisternal maturation model
Secreted protein remains in cisterna (red arrows)
Cisterna “matures” as Golgi enzymes move backward

TRANS-GOLGI NETWORK
Vesicles sorted to trans-Golgi, lysosome, endosome, or cell membrane

EXOCYTOSIS
Signal sequence for transmembrane proteins – YXXF binds to AP1 complex of clathrin coat
Soluble proteins secreted unless sent elsewhere
Constitutive or regulated secretion
